risk_if_not_implemented: Without Cloud Security Architecture, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Secure Engineering Principles
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to facilitate the implementation of industry-recognized security, compliance and resilience practices in the specification, design, development, implementation and modification of Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS).
compensating_control_1.justification: Secure Engineering Principles (SEA-01) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Cloud Security Architecture (CLD-02) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Network Segmentation (macrosegementation)
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to ensure network architecture utilizes network segmentation to isolate Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS) to protect from other network resources.
compensating_control_2.justification: Network Segmentation (macrosegementation) (NET-06) provides network-level access restriction that compensates for the absence of Cloud Security Architecture (CLD-02) by limiting attacker reach and lateral movement opportunities across the environment. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
